methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $18M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Dendra Systems is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Susan Graham and Irina Fedorenko that has raised $18M to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector includes one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables—zero Series A-stage peers and zero similar-tier datapoints emerged from filtering—making a reliable peer-median multiple approach inapplicable.

Given data constraints, stage-progression analysis provides the primary valuation anchor. Using the $18M total raised as a value proxy and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the subsequent stage yields an implied valuation of $36M. This single-method result is treated conservatively: the low-end estimate of $31M reflects downside sensitivity, while the high-end of $41M accommodates modest variance around the step-up assumption.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $31M to $41M, with $36M as the median point. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-stage peer validation and reliance on a single stage-progression method. Any Series A re-price or follow-on funding data for sector peers would materially strengthen confidence in this range. Investors should treat this as a preliminary framework pending more granular market comparables or detailed unit economics from the company.
